title: I am hoping for Thebes
subtitle: Desire encoded as ritual language; symbolic invocation of order after collapse
description: An archetypal and symbolic invocation documenting infrastructure thinking entering myth. It captures a preparation for succession or emergence following the collapse of a current order, expressed through imperative and ritualized declarations.

motifMap.meaning: An ordered place of power, meaning, and structure; the object of directional longing.
The axis of collapse; the end of the current order that necessitates ritual preparation.

excerpt: I am hoping for Thebes. Don the headless. Anoint in oil. Prepare the slaves. The pharaoh is dead.

sourceInputs: Phase Analysis 3.0 — “I am hoping for Thebes”

integrity.notes: Generated based on Phase Analysis 3.0. Identified as a pure canonical expression of Totem Current without variant per user instruction. Family set to Aftermath / Purification / Recurrence.
